diary:
Woke up to that soft, constant rain like the city was running a quiet metronome. At home in Woodinville I did my usual plant check—just a glance, a little reassurance—and then made coffee while I looked over my day plan. I packed my rain layer and umbrella without even thinking, because the drizzle didn’t feel like something that would “maybe” stop. 

The drive toward Mercer Island was slower than usual after SR-520 closed eastbound from 9 to noon. I made extra buffer time, and sure enough, by the time I reached the Mercer Island Community Center the traffic had turned into that gray, bumper-to-bumper crawl. Inside, I stayed calm with tax-season reconciliations—double-checking numbers, no rushing. Lunch at QFC kept me warm and dry, and later Whole Foods in Totem Lake felt like a quick reset for rain-friendly supplies. 

After work I cycled at Juanita Bay Park once the weather finally eased, and even the wet air felt lighter. Dinner with my friend at Matt’s in the Market rounded it out, and I went to bed feeling grateful and steady.
thought_process: I planned my morning around the SR-520 closure, not because I love traffic, but because I hate being late. The steady rain from 7am to 2pm also nudged me toward indoor stops—QFC for warmth, then Whole Foods for practical stuff—so I wouldn’t waste energy later. At the Mercer Island Community Center, I chose a slow, careful pace on reconciliations because tax-season work punishes shortcuts; today the world felt noisy enough, so I didn’t need to add mistakes to it. My mood stayed even through the commute stress, then lifted after the closure window when roads loosened up. By the time I got to Juanita Bay Park for an easy cycle, I could feel the day switching from “manage” to “move.”
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
